headings.usage: Used for CRT console text, section headers in the config panel, and phone keypad numbers to give a technical/analog vibe.

body.usage: Used for configuration settings, long-form text, and secondary UI elements for optimal readability.

strategy.simulator: Deep bakelite black casing, glowing CRT green/amber for screens and indicator lights. High contrast.
strategy.dashboard: High-contrast Swiss brutalist/terminal approach. Dark surface with stark borders and monospace green accents.

surfaces.name: Retro Terminal (CRT)
surfaces.description: Deep black background, glowing green text, with a faint scanline repeating-linear-gradient overlay.

surfaces.name: Tactile Buttons (Keypad/Toggles)
surfaces.description: Hard shadow offsets (e.g., 2-4px solid border bottom/right). When pressed, translate down and remove shadow to simulate mechanical switches.

borders_and_shadows.shadows: shadow-[4px_4px_0px_0px_rgba(57,255,20,0.2)] for active neon elements, standard offset shadow for buttons

visual_enhancers.textures: Use scanlines for the CRT transcript and subtle grain on the main app background to remove digital flatness.

grids_and_layouts.app_structure: Split screen or side-by-side. Left/Center: The interactive Bakelite Phone Simulator. Right: The Config Panel.
grids_and_layouts.simulator: Absolute or heavily constrained relative layout mimicking physical hardware dimensions. Asymmetrical positioning of phone elements (handset, dial pad).
grids_and_layouts.config_dashboard: The 'Bento' High Density Grid. Dense, functional, tight gaps (gap-4). Every pixel utilized for switches and inputs.
grids_and_layouts.spacing: Generous spacing around the phone simulator to let it breathe (p-12). Dense padding inside config cards (p-4 to p-6). Container with border MUST have padding.

components.name: Phone Keypad (DTMF)
components.rules: Must feel mechanical. Use hard borders, sharp corners, and deep press animations. Include visual feedback when 'pressed'.

components.name: CRT Transcript Console
components.rules: Dark background, monospace green text, subtle text-shadow for glow. Overflow scroll with hidden scrollbar. Must include pseudo-element scanlines.

components.name: Config Panel Controls
components.rules: Use Shadcn logic but heavily override styles to look like analog toggle switches and physical sliders. Hard contrasting colors. No transparent backgrounds over content.

motion_and_interactions.buttons: Active state: `active:translate-y-[2px] active:shadow-none` for mechanical click feel.
motion_and_interactions.crt_screen: Subtle flicker animation on power-up or program switch. Slower load times artificially to mimic retro tech.
motion_and_interactions.indicators: Message/Ringer light pulses using opacity keyframes and blur variations (`animate-pulse` mixed with drop-shadow).

accessibility.contrast: Ensure glowing text meets 4.5:1 ratio against black backgrounds. Use text-shadow to increase legibility if needed.
accessibility.motion: Respect `prefers-reduced-motion` for CRT flickers and overlays.
accessibility.data_testid: ALL interactive components MUST have `data-testid` attributes (e.g., `keypad-button-1`, `config-toggle-fortune`).

icons.style: Technical, unrounded edges if possible. Keep them minimal.

images.description: Black rotary phone for aesthetic inspiration or hero background overlay in the config welcome screen.
Green phosphor texture. To be blended (`mix-blend-overlay` with low opacity) over the CRT transcript area to give it an authentic screen feel.

libraries.3d: Not required. A highly polished 2D UI with tactile shadow offsets and CSS animations will sell the retro hardware aesthetic better than heavy 3D. Keep it performant.
libraries.animations: framer-motion for tactile hardware switch states and CRT boot sequences.

instructions_to_main_agent: Install required dependencies: `yarn add framer-motion lucide-react`.
Set up Tailwind variables for dark mode strictly. Use `#0a0a0a` for the main background.
Create the main layout as a split view: Simulator on the left/center, Config panel on the right (or as an expandable drawer).
For the Phone Simulator: Build it entirely with pure HTML/Tailwind (.jsx) to create the overlapping, asymmetrical 'hardware' look. Do not use generic Shadcn cards for the phone body.
For the Config Dashboard: Heavy use of customized Shadcn components (forms, inputs, select) but strip Vercel-like gloss. Apply 'Tactile' retro styling (hard borders, monospace labels).
Implement the CRT Transcript screen with `overflow-y-auto`, a green text shadow, and a subtle CSS scanline overlay.
NEVER use generic `Inter` or `sans` for headings. Ensure `IBM Plex Mono` or `Source Code Pro` is fetched from Google Fonts and injected.
Ensure the header has a solid or heavily blurred background, NEVER fully transparent.
universal_guidelines: You tend to converge toward generic, 'on distribution' outputs. Avoid this: make creative, distinctive frontends that surprise and delight.
Dark colors look good when used independently without gradients. DO NOT use dark colors as gradients.
Don't make generic centered layouts. Create depth through layered design elements with z-index hierarchy.
You MUST NOT apply universal transition (e.g., `transition: all`). Always add transitions for specific interactive elements.
You MUST NOT center align the app container, i.e. do not add `.App { text-align: center; }`.
Every interaction needs micro-animations. Static = dead.
Use 2-3x more spacing than feels comfortable.
Subtle grain textures, noise overlays, custom cursors, and loading animations separate good from extraordinary.
All interactive elements MUST include a `data-testid` attribute (e.g. `data-testid='handset-toggle'`).
